title: Rapid DNA Extraction from Sediment Using Spin Columns
author: Linda Armbrecht

steps.instruction: Wipe the outside of the tubes with 80 % ethanol. wipe especially along the rim of the tubes, as they are the most prone to contamination label your tubes clearly again, in case the labeling has been removed from the ethanol
Incubate over night on rotator (your tutors will do this 24 hrs prior to the prac). 10 rpm, Room temperature, , 17 hrs
Add 500 µL lysis buffer to your sample (your tutors will do this 24 hrs prior to the prac). Sample %s %s
Lysis General advice Use best practise pipetting techniques (e.g., wear gloves, avoid contamination)

Centrifuge the tubes for 2 minutes at 16,400 g to separate the lysate from undigested sample material. Before starting step 1.4: clean the centrifuge rotor surface with a bit of ethanol ensure the centrifuge is balanced (i.e., an even number of tubes, each tube contains the same volume of liquid, tubes are arranged opposite of each other) ensure the lid is on the rotor before closing the centrifuge and turning it on

Transfer the lysate to a fresh 1.7 mL tube. Be careful not to transfer any sediment. it's useful to place the pipette over the lowest point of the pellet while you're taking up the supernatant, do not touch the pellet pipette slowly and continuously
Add 1200 µL of Solution C4 (50 - 70 % guanidinhydrochlorid + 1 - 10 % isopropylalcohol) to the supernatant and vortex for 5 s.
Loading procedure (3 x) of the spin column
Load the first 675 µL of the supernatant onto an Spin Column and centrifuge. 10000 x g, °C, , room temperature, 1 min. Discard the flow-through.
Load the second 675 µL of the supernatant onto an Spin Column and centrifuge. 10000 x g, °C, , room temperature, 1 min. Discard the flow-through.
Load the rest of the supernatant onto an Spin Column and centrifuge. 10000 x g, °C, , room temperature, 1 min. Discard the flow-through.
Removing any contaminants / residues from the filter membrane
Add 500 µL of Solution C5 (= 70 % ethanol) to the spin column.
Centrifuge the spin column and discard the flow-through. 10000 x g, °C, , 30 s, room temperature
Centrifuge the spin column again and discard the flow-through with the collection tube. 10000 x g, °C, , 1 min, room temperature

Carefully place the spin column in a clean 1.7 mL tube. Avoid splashing any Solution C5 onto the spin column. don't touch the spin column itself (risk of contamination), only the rim (top of column where the lid is)
Elute the bound DNA from the filter membrane.

Add 25 µL of Elution Buffer (EB / C6 buffer) to the center of the white filter membrane, let sit for 2 minutes. It's important to double check whether the membrane filter has fully soaked up all of the 25 µL of C6 buffer. Aim to get the elution buffer into the centre of the filter membrane, but avoid touching the filter membrane with the pipette tip! Stick to the incubation time. Incubating for a bit longer is okay, but you shouldn't incubate for a shorter period of time.
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, , 30 s and discard the Spin Column but keep the tube containing your extracted DNA!

Combine the replicate sediment DNA extracts in one tube. Leave the control extract in a separate tube. 1 x tube with ~ 75 µL of transparent DNA extract --> ready for downstream applications 1 x tube with the extraction control
Store your DNA extract and control (clearly labeled in a rack or cryo box) in the freezer at -20 °C until the next prac.
Vortex briefly to mix sediments with lysis buffer (your tutors will do this 24 hrs prior to the prac).
